dsls.markdown:
# Welcome! ## Welcome to Teaching Computer Science Discoveries! #### This free self-paced professional learning is designed for educators who want to get started teaching Computer Science Discoveries. After completing these online modules, you'll be ready to start teaching CS Discoveries with your students. ### **You will:** * #### Learn how to navigate the Code.org website and teacher tools. * #### Set up your course in the Code.org learning platform. * #### Gain a high level understanding of the content, tools, and pedagogy included in CS Discoveries. * #### Prepare to begin teaching CS Discoveries with your students.
#### **Suggested time to complete the course:** 3 hours
## Take the Pre-Survey #### Code.org values and is responsive to feedback. Please fill out this short pre-survey to tell us about who you are and how we can best support you. Take the Pre-Survey ___ ## Module 1 #### You will begin with a module focused on orienting you to Code.org, CS Discoveries, and this professional learning experience. ### **In Module 1, you will:** * #### Get an introduction to Code.org and CS Discoveries. * #### Learn how to engage in this professional learning course.
#### **Suggested time to complete this module:** 10 minutes

# Receiving a Certificate for this Module #### Upon finishing this module, you will be eligible to receive a certificate of completion similar to the one above. You must complete **every level** in this module to access your certificate. #### We encourage you to share your certificates to social media to celebrate your success! ## FAQ ### **How do I complete a level in a self-paced module?** #### Completion varies by level type: * #### On a text-only level (such as this one), you'll need to click the "Continue" button. * #### For a coding activity level, you may be asked to complete a task. Some levels will require a correct answer. You will have an unlimited number of attempts. * #### For a checking for understanding level, such as vocabulary matching or multiple choice, you will need to answer correctly. You will have an unlimited number of attempts. * #### For an open reflection level, you will need to submit an answer to the question(s) provided.
### **When will I receive my certificate?** #### You'll be taken to a congratulations page once you've completed every level within a self-paced experience. If the self-paced experience has multiple units (see the image directly below), you will see the congratulations page once every level is complete in every unit. #### This self-paced experience has two units: Getting Started with Code.org and Preparing to Teach Problem Solving and Computing.
### **Where can I find the certificates I've earned?** #### You can find all your certificates at studio.code.org/my-professional-learning. Your self-paced certificates will be in the “My Self Paced Professional Learning Courses” section in the Actions column once you have completed the module. #### You can also access your certificates by clicking "Print Certificate" on each module overview page. ### **Why can't I access my certificate?** #### Ensure that *every* level has been completed in the module. Your module overview page should look similar to this image: #### You may need to return to individual levels to complete them. If every level is complete and you still can't access a certificate, send us a message.

https://studio.code.org/s/self-paced-pl-.long_instructions: ## Please watch this video featuring Code.org's founder and CEO, Hadi Partovi.

dsls.markdown:
# About Code.org > Code.org is a nonprofit dedicated to **expanding access to computer science** in schools and increasing participation by young women and students from other underrepresented groups. #### Code.org's vision for CS education is that **every student in every school** has the opportunity to learn computer science. In order to make this vision a reality, Code.org: * #### Builds free computer science curriculum. * #### Trains teachers to teach computer science. * #### Advocates for policy change in support of CS Education. ## Access to Computer Science Education #### More important than the tech workforce pipeline, **computer science is now a foundational subject for all 21st century careers,** making access to this knowledge an equity issue that must be addressed. **A student's opportunity should not be determined by the color of their skin, their family income, or the neighborhood they grow up in.** Access to computer science education is improving, but in many regions or the United States, disparities in access still exist.
#### Click here and select your state handout to learn more about computer science access in your region.

https://studio.code.org/s/self-paced-pl-.long_instructions: ## Watch this video to hear tips from current CS Discoveries teachers.

dsls.markdown:
# About CS Discoveries

CS Discoveries is Code.org's computer science curriculum designed for 6 - 10th grade students. It is mapped to CSTA standards and covers topics such as artificial intelligence, problem solving, programming, physical computing, user centered design, and data, while inspiring students as they build their own websites, apps, animations, games, and physical computing systems.


## Why should you teach CS Discoveries? * #### **Engaging Curriculum:** Our team designed the CS Discoveries curriculum to support students and teachers new to the discipline. The curriculum includes daily lesson plans made up of inquiry-based activities, videos, assessments, and computing tools, allowing teachers to guide and learn alongside students as they discover core computing concepts throughout the course.
* #### **A Focus on Emerging Technologies:** The CS Discoveries curriculum features a unit on artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML). As these technologies touch more and more of our daily lives, they have become an essential part of foundational computer science education. The unit focuses on AI ethics, examines issues of bias, and explores and explains fundamental concepts through a number of online and offline activities and full-group discussions.
* #### **Extensive Teacher Resources:** The CS Discoveries curriculum includes daily lesson plans along with integrated discussion goals, teaching tips, and assessment opportunities, our lesson plans come with detailed pacing instructions, activity guides, exemplar projects, sample marked rubrics, and more! Teachers are also able to create and manage classroom sections, check student progress, administer assessments, and integrate with Google or Clever classroom, all from a Teacher Dashboard! (Sign in required.)

https://studio.code.org/s/self-paced-pl-.long_instructions: ## Watch this video to hear tips from current CS Discoveries teachers.

dsls.markdown:
# About This Professional Learning Course

This professional learning opportunity is divided into four modules. Each module focuses on a different aspect of preparing to teach CS Discoveries for the first time.

## Module 1: Welcome - ⭐️ You're here! #### This module is a short introduction to both Code.org and this professional learning course. ### **You will:** * #### Get an introduction to Code.org and CS Discoveries. * #### Learn how to engage in this professional learning course.
#### **Suggested Time:** 10 minutes ___ ## Module 2: Navigating Code.org #### This module is designed to orient you to the tools you will use to support student learning while teaching CS Discoveries. ### **You will:** * #### Navigate the Code.org website and teacher tools. * #### Set up a classroom section * #### Locate and use course lesson plans and teacher resources. * #### Learn how to access support while teaching the course.
#### **Suggested Time:** 70 minutes ___ ## Module 3: Overview of CS Discoveries #### This module will provide you with an overview of the CS Discoveries course with an emphasis on concepts students will learn and tools students will use throughout the course. Engaging in this module will help you to make decisions about how to select the course content that you will implement in your classroom. ### **You will:** * #### Gain a high-level understanding of the CS Discoveries curriculum. * #### Become familiar with the programming environments used within CS Discoveries. * #### Decide which units of CS Discoveries you will teach in your class.
#### **Suggested time:** 60 minutes ___ ## Module 4: Teaching and Learning CS Discoveries #### In this module, you will be introduced to authentic CS practices that students will engage with throughout the CS Discoveries course. These practices include debugging, using resources, and pair programming. You will also explore the role of the teacher within a CS Discoveries classroom and make a plan for how you will support student learning while teaching the course. ### **You will:** * #### Consider the role of the teacher in a CS Discoveries classroom. * #### Learn about authentic CS practices that students will develop throughout the CS Discoveries course. * #### Make a plan for how you will support student learning through differentiation, ongoing assessment, and promoting a culture of inclusion.
#### **Suggested time:** 50 minutes

# Types of Activities Found in This Course

You will encounter a variety of different types of activities referred to as levels. These levels are located in the Code.org learning platform called Code Studio. Each level is designed to help you prepare to teach CS Discoveries for the first time.

#### Read the information below to learn more about the different types of levels you will encounter in these professional learning modules.
## Read About a Topic #### **New information about a topic is presented in text based levels.** These levels are designed to: #### - Provide information about the course. #### - Introduce new topics. #### - Connect you with resources. #### Many of these levels have dropdown arrows that contain additional details about a topic. Some levels contain links to resources that exist outside of this professional learning course.
## Watch a Video #### - **Teacher tips:** Hear CS Discoveries teachers share their experience and tips for teaching CS Discoveries. #### - **Getting stared:** Follow along with guided tours of the Code.org website and important features. #### - **Curriculum videos:** Watch videos that are included in the CS Discoveries curriculum.
## Complete a Task #### Follow step by step instructions to try something new. These tasks will help you: #### - Navigate to curriculum, resources, and support. #### - Explore course content. #### We recommend that you **complete these tasks in a new tab** so you can quickly return to this professional learning course when you have completed the task.
## Choose to explore #### Choice levels are intended to provide you with options for digging deeper through exploring various features within the CS Discoveries course. These explorations are considered optional. **You may choose to complete all, some, or none of the activities within the choice level.** Each option within the choice level contains a short description of what you will explore.
## Make a Plan #### Throughout the course, you will encounter opportunities to make a plan for your classroom. These prompts are designed to help you think through decisions you will likely need to make before your first day of class. Your answers will be stored in the course for you to revisit at any time. **You might also consider using a journal or notebook to document your plans and ideas as you work through this professional learning course.**
## Check Your Understanding #### Quick check for understanding questions are included throughout this professional learning course. You can **use these to make sure you are on the right track** and understanding key points presented in this course. If you have a difficult time answering the questions, consider going back to explore more of the content and resources presented in the levels prior to the check for understanding question.

# Navigating This Professional Learning Course

###**Find the learning that works for you** * #### **Go at your own pace:** This self-paced course is designed to put you in the driver's seat, and give you control over your own learning. Suggested times are given for each module and lesson. However, we encourage you to engage with the content at your own pace. * #### **Pick your content:** The content of this professional learning course is organized in the suggested progression for teachers new to both Code.org and CS Discoveries. However, you can engage with the content in the order that best meets your needs. Select an option below to begin.

## Choose Your Path ### **Option A:** Click here to return to the Course Overview page to select your next module. ### **Option B:** Use the table below to navigate directly to the content of your choice.
Module 1
Welcome 		Module 2
Navigating Code.org 		Module 3
